result:
Build green. No `eprintln` instrumentation was added during this debug — I diagnosed by reading the source directly.

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-lex/src/lib.rs:1935` `str2num_stub` only tried `i64::from_str_radix(...,16)`, `parse::<i64>()`, and `parse::<f64>()`. Rust's `f64::parse` does not accept hex floats, so `0x7.4` (a valid Lua hex float literal at `reference/lua-c/testes/math.lua:435`) fell through all three branches and returned `None`, triggering the "malformed number" lexer error. The hex-float parser is already fully ported as `lua_vm::object::str2num` (`crates/lua-vm/src/object.rs:566`) — the lexer just wasn't using it.

**Fix:** In `read_numeral` (lua-lex/src/lib.rs:~1120), replaced the call to the local `str2num_stub` with `lua_vm::object::str2num(num_bytes, &mut obj)` and matched on the resulting `LuaValue::Int(_)` / `LuaValue::Float(_)`.

**New state:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` succeeds with only pre-existing warnings. Did not re-run the test program per instructions; the mega-loop will re-test. The unrelated line-number-reporting drift (435 reported as 443) is a separate issue — left unchanged.
